top_categories.workout_context: At mile 60 of 100, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 100 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
Axel Merckx Gran Fondo's mixed terrain rewards steady threshold power. Surging and recovering costs more watts than just holding.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Axel Merckx Gran Fondo's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before July.
When someone attacks on mixed terrain, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
100 miles rewards fat oxidation. This volume work shifts your fuel mix so you're still burning fat at hour 5, not bonking at hour 3.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Axel Merckx Gran Fondo. Train it until it feels boring.
At Axel Merckx Gran Fondo, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Axel Merckx Gran Fondo's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 100 miles.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 3,937ft of climbing on mixed terrain does to your power file on race day.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 100 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.
Varied gradients on mixed terrain demand climbing versatility. Sit the shallow stuff, stand the steep stuff, switch without thinking.

race_overlay.heat: July in Penticton, British Columbia, Canada. Begin heat acclimatization 10–14 days before Axel Merckx Gran Fondo — 20–30min sauna sessions or midday rides in full kit. Pre-load sodium 48 hours out. Target 500–750ml/hr with electrolytes on race day.
race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Axel Merckx Gran Fondo’s 100 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at Axel Merckx Gran Fondo. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, Endurance, and TT Threshold to prepare you for 100 miles of mixed terrain in Penticton, British Columbia, Canada.
